terms.definition: Auto-formalization is the process of translating ambiguous natural language reasoning into precise, symbolic code that a computer solver can execute with mathematical certainty. The 'Draft-and-Prune' method improves this by generating multiple potential logical drafts and systematically discarding those that are non-executable or logically inconsistent.
terms.analogy: In a high-end kitchen, a sous-chef might prep five different versions of a complex sauce (Drafting), but the Head Chef tastes them all and throws away the four that don't meet the exact flavor profile (Pruning) before the plate ever reaches the customer.
terms.business_value: This technology bridges the gap between 'hallucinating' chatbots and reliable business logic. For CEOs, this means AI can finally be trusted with high-stakes financial or legal workflows where 'almost correct' isn't good enough.

terms.definition: This architectural pattern involves attaching specialized monitoring modules (sidecars) to AI agents to track the timing and 'geometry' of how tasks move through a network. These sidecars detect when a delegation of tasks is likely to fail or 'cascade' into an error, allowing the system to switch routing logic before a total system shutdown occurs.
terms.analogy: Think of it as a car’s Electronic Stability Control (ESC) sensors that sit alongside the wheels; they don't drive the car, but they monitor the 'geometry' of the turn and intervene instantly if they detect the vehicle is about to spin out of control.
terms.business_value: As companies move from single chatbots to complex multi-agent 'swarms,' system stability is the new bottleneck. Sidecar routing ensures that one failing agent doesn't paralyze your entire automated operations infrastructure.

terms.definition: Unlike traditional AI memory that simply retrieves text snippets, this architecture stores information as a web of interconnected facts (a graph). 'Belief Revision' is the formal logic used to update this web when new, conflicting information is learned, ensuring the AI maintains a consistent and non-contradictory understanding of a situation over time.
terms.analogy: Imagine a kitchen pantry that doesn't just store ingredients, but understands their relationships; if you tell the system the milk has spoiled, it doesn't just 'remember' that fact—it 'revises its belief' and automatically removes all recipes requiring milk from your suggested dinner list.
terms.business_value: This allows for 'True Personalization.' It enables AI agents to maintain a coherent, long-term memory of a client's evolving preferences or a project's shifting requirements, preventing the repetitive or contradictory interactions that frustrate high-value customers.
